text: GPU shaders are small programs that run on graphics hardware to determine vertex positions (vertex shaders) and pixel colors (fragment shaders).
GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language) and HLSL (High-Level Shading Language) are the two dominant shader languages used in game development.
Fragment shaders execute in massive parallelism across GPU cores, with modern GPUs running thousands of shader invocations simultaneously to achieve real-time frame rates at high resolutions.
